# Transcrição: 01-Overview of Facebook Ads - Inside the Ad Account 

- **Fonte:** 01-Overview of Facebook Ads - Inside the Ad Account 
- **Duração:** 11:45
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:05]

Welcome to the first training inside of this Facebook ads for beginners course. So we're going to take it right back to the basics and what I'm going to do over the next rollout of videos is I'm going to take you through from how to set things up and understanding how Facebook ads work, how to create your own campaigns, and you'll also find some advanced training in here as well. So my aim is that after this course you'll be confident in terms of creating your business manager, accessing client accounts, rolling out a campaign, creating some really good creative and copy for your ads, the ad formats, all of this stuff, just overall very confident and comfortable when it comes to running Facebook ads. So in this module what I want to show you is the premise of Facebook ads and how everything sort of fits together. So as you can see here I am in a Facebook news feed. This should look pretty common to you. Now here's where things get a little bit confusing for new Facebook marketers. So when you have a personal profile such as this right now, you have your profile and your own news feed and you will have one ad account tied to this. So you can create ads and manage ads. So if you click on this little drop down here, if you've never seen your ad account associated to your personal profile, it's right here. So if I click on this it's going to take me to the ad account associated to this personal profile. Makes sense so far, right? So typically a lot of new Facebook marketers would have run all their ads from this one ad account and they access this ad account from the personal profile. So you would log into Facebook and you would click on here and go manage ads. Sometimes it shows up here as well. So that's pretty standard. However when you start working with clients you don't want to have just one ad account and you also want to be able to manage all of the different ad accounts and pages of your clients without having to do that from your personal profile. If I have 20 clients you can just imagine how cluttered this would all be, right? So it would be really, really cluttered over here. So this is essentially why we need to create business manager. Business manager is just a platform that allows us to manage client ad accounts, our team, so any contractors or staff that we have on our team, and client pages which brings me to another important point. Pages

## [03:05 - 06:07]

so if I go here, cat howl, this page, this is a business page, this is very different to the ad account. So this is a completely separate entity to this ad account here. Meaning I can run ads to my page from any ad accounts that I have access to, and I can also run ads to lots of different pages from one ad account. So they're not mutually exclusive. This is something that a lot of new marketers get tied up with is they think a page is tied to or married to one specific ad account when in reality they're two completely separate things. So that's a really important foundation to understand. Now as you can see here, I'm inside of an ad account and I'm just going to quickly walk you through just so you can get comfortable in terms of the look and the feel here. So you'll see here the new setup that Facebook recently rolled out is you've got your ad account overview tab here. So Facebook's actually made even more updates to the account overview tab. So I'll just talk you guys through some of the cool things that you can do here and then I'll let cat get back to the rest of the ad account. So firstly you've got the overview tab here which at a glance you can see the most important metrics to you. So by toggling these little down arrows here you can select whatever metric you'd like. So you can see here what the total conversion value is against the amount spent and how many purchases have occurred and you can quickly see where those purchases have occurred or how your spend has changed throughout the period that you're tracking for. So right now this one's set to one month as you can see up here and then further down you can see what campaign objectives you've run over that period that you're looking at. And at a glance you can quickly see the result that you've had from each of those objectives, the cost per result, the reach and how much you've spent in each one and how that has changed to the period before that. So you'll notice for the conversion objective campaigns, the product catalog as well as conversions down here, it's not actually showing the results and the cost per result. That's because they're using different conversion objectives. So the two product catalog sales campaigns, one might be optimizing for purchases and one might be optimizing for add to cart and because of that Facebook can't actually report on one specific metric. Further down you've got breakdowns based on age and gender as well as time of day. So over here you've got age and gender together, so women and men and then the age breakdowns. But if you wanted to see those in further detail you could break it down by age in particular

## [06:10 - 09:11]

or by gender as well. And then you can actually toggle these two to whatever metrics are most important to you. Same thing by hour, time of day, you can see very quickly where your spend is going versus where your purchases or leads or whatever it is that you're looking to get is coming in. And then also after that we've got the location breakdown. So you can see it broken down by country, by region or by a designated market area region. So that's even smaller than regions which are typically states or areas within a country. And then you can toggle this once again to whatever is most important to you, whether you want to track where the leads are coming in from or where the purchases are coming in from or where your spend is going. Secondly you've got the creative reporting tab over here where you can see all of the ad creative that you've got running and how many ads they're being used in as well as the delivery for those ads whether they're active or not, what results they're getting you, how much reach you've had and your cost per result. And you can quickly see all of the different metrics for the ad creative variations that you're running. You've got this tab here which is your campaigns. So these are all the different campaigns we're running. And make sure you watch that video around the difference between campaigns, ad sets and ads. For the moment I'm just going to talk you through just so you can see the back end of an actual ad account and what it all looks like. Then you've got ad sets which is where you set your targeting and your budgets for your different campaigns. And then you've got ads. Now let's say I've got a campaign here and I want to see all the different ad sets that are under this one particular campaign. One of the things that I can do is I can either tick this and then click on the ad sets and you'll see here it's changed to ad sets for one campaign. And it will bring up all the ad sets I had under this campaign. I could also just click on this. There you go. And the same thing for different ad sets. If I want to see all the different ads under this ad set I would click on here and you can see all the different creatives or we could just click on it. If I want to see all the ads for lots of them you just click on them and then go like this. So with campaigns you'll have one campaign that represents a stage in your funnel. At the ad set level you can have multiple ad sets for one campaign. So you'll see this is one campaign but we've got four or five different ad sets in here and it's split between countries, US mobile, AU mobile, AU desktop. So it's split between placements and countries as well. So this is one way to split tests. So you can

## [09:11 - 11:45]

have lots of different ad sets under one campaign and again you can have lots and lots of different ads under the same ad set. So you'll see here this one's got four, this one's got two. You can also use the same exact ads under all of these ads sets. So this is how an ad account is structured. You've also got your reporting column here and watch the video on how to pull up reporting properly. So I talk you through how to do customized columns and what to pull up through there. But you can essentially pull up different stats through this column. So you can see link clicks, click through rates that happen, all of this good stuff. If you've got videos, video engagement, etc, etc. If we go up here, what you'll see is you've got access to all of the tools that you need to use. So over here we've got audience insights. This is probably the best replacement for the old Facebook graph tool where you can get some really cool insights in terms of how to target what your audience is into, similar pages, all of this great stuff. You've got page posts. This is where you can create page posts. So if you're running social proof or you want to run the same ad, now there's training around this under the Facebook ad types. You've got your ads reporting, you've got custom conversions, watch the training on tracking, analytics, your audiences. So here's where you create all your targeting for your custom audiences and lookalike audiences. And then your assets, so images, product catalogs. This is a little bit more advanced, this type of stuff here. You've also got your ad account setting. So here's where you can change the name. You've got your billing. So here's where you would go add the credit card information and you can pull up your invoices and then obviously business settings. So this is business manager right now. But this is what this will all look like for you once you've got that set up. So this is essentially a back end view at an ad account.
